comment: Each family lists 2-4 candidate moods. Macro mood byte 0 picks among them via byte0 % len(candidates). Sub-flavor (byte 1) further differentiates. Rationales are mnemonic, not strict: the goal is that file flavor predicts a recognizable palette.

text-code.rationale: Code reads as structured/grid-like: synthwave arpeggios, techno repetition, glitch/IDM micro-edits, deep-house pulse all evoke 'machine logic'.

image.rationale: Static visual -> contemplative palettes: ambient pads, downtempo, ballad, synthwave-pastel.

audio.rationale: Already-musical files map to overtly musical, song-form moods: hip-hop, latin, house, ballad.

video.rationale: Moving picture -> cinematic, energetic propulsion: trailer, DnB, synthwave, techno.

archive.rationale: Archives are 'compressed/dense' -> glitch, boom-bap (chopped/sampled aesthetic), techno, downtempo.

document-pdf.rationale: Long-form reading material -> ballad, ambient, downtempo, cinematic underscore.

spreadsheet.rationale: Grids and rows -> four-on-the-floor pulses (techno/house), synthwave, glitch.

executable.rationale: Compiled binaries: machine, dense -> techno, glitch/IDM, synthwave, DnB.

font.rationale: Fonts are typographic, refined -> ambient, ballad, synthwave, downtempo.

model-3d.rationale: Spatial/sculptural -> cinematic, ambient drones, synthwave, techno.

web-asset.rationale: Web/UI artifacts -> house (lounge), synthwave, glitch, lo-fi hip-hop.

database.rationale: Stores of data -> techno regularity, glitch, ambient, downtempo.

unknown.rationale: Unknown content stays musically safe and ambient-leaning to avoid mismatched expectations.
